Concurrent command issuers can race on queue depth unless host serialization is enforced

dependencies: Core docs consistently connect CV pipeline modules to protocol/scheduler/serial interfaces
OpenSpec mirrors are central dependency anchors for contracts/configs/gui layout
Bench and GUI runtime surfaces depend on stable queue, frame, and trigger semantics
conflicts_or_gaps: 9 of 14 requested layered files are missing in repository root (all non-core docs except agents.md)
SAFE mode, queue lifecycle, and retry semantics are documented in protocol.md but lack dedicated state/error/security model linkages
No explicit doc-level mapping from servo timing budgets to SCHED trigger guarantees
performance_bottlenecks: Queue depth cap (8) and BUSY behavior can throttle burst trigger throughput
Static retry/backoff (100 ms timeout, 3 retries) may underperform on variable-latency transports
Lack of explicit threading model increases risk of frame/queue contention under load
safety_correctness_concerns: SAFE mode transition policy exists, but missing security/state/error documents reduce assurance for fault handling
Absence of correctness-layer docs may weaken deterministic validation across frame parsing, queue operations, and trigger scheduling
Spec/runtime parity is declared but not coupled to a mandatory automated consistency gate
